strategy.name: EMA(20)/EMA(50) Trend + RSI(14) Pullback Strategy
strategy.description: Trend-following swing strategy for 4H candles. EMA(20)/EMA(50) crossover defines trend direction, RSI(14) times entries on pullbacks within that trend.

indicators.ema_50: Slow trend line. EMA(20) above EMA(50) = uptrend. Below = downtrend.
indicators.rsi_14: Standard 14-period RSI. Used to time pullback entries within the established trend, not for reversals.

long: EMA(20) is above EMA(50) — uptrend confirmed
RSI(14) has dropped below 40 — pullback within the uptrend, entry on snap-back
short: EMA(20) is below EMA(50) — downtrend confirmed
RSI(14) has risen above 60 — pullback within the downtrend, entry on snap-back

exit_rules: Hard stop: 2% below entry for longs, 2% above entry for shorts
Take profit: 4% above entry for longs, 4% below entry for shorts (2:1 reward/risk)
Alternative exit: EMA(20) crosses back against the trade direction
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Paper trade first — validate this strategy before going live
notes: Swing/trend-following strategy designed for 4H candles. Do not apply to timeframes below 1H — RSI(14) and EMA periods need enough candles to be meaningful.
